rationale: Direct directional alignment. Safety/security controls should prohibit harmful generation and distribution of abusive synthetic media.
Strong directional alignment. Consent and notice controls support privacy-protection aspects of synthetic media laws.
Strong alignment. Incident management is the right operational control for responding to reports of AI-enabled abuse.
Strong alignment. This maps to misuse, prompt abuse, impersonation, evasion, and harmful content-generation vectors.
Partial alignment. Verification supports detection and provenance checks but state deepfake statutes often focus on conduct and consent rather than technical watermarking.
